clean_text: السلام الله بسم الله الرحم الرحمن الرحيم الحمد لله والصلاه والسلام على رسول الله واشهد ان لا اله الا الله واشهد ان محمدا عبده ورسوله اللهم صل وسلم وبارك على نبينا محمد وعلى اله واصحابه summary: This lecture discusses the hadith of Anas ibn Malik regarding the Prophet Muhammad's method of performing wudu (ablution), specifically focusing on the proper technique of combing through the beard (takhliyal-lihya) and interlacing the fingers and toes during ablution. The speaker explains that the Prophet would take a handful of water and run it through his beard, stating 'This is how my Lord commanded me.' The lecture delves into the fiqh (Islamic jurisprudence) principles governing whether beard combing is obligatory or recommended, discussing when the skin is visible versus when the beard is dense, and emphasizing the importance of ensuring water reaches all parts of the body during ablution.
suggested_title: The Prophet's Method of Combing the Beard and Interlacing Fingers During Ablution
catchy_title: How to Properly Wash Between Your Fingers in Wudu
